title: MGET
description: Atomically returns the string values of one or more keys.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to retrieve.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information. Returns the values of all specified keys. For every key that does not hold a string value or does not exist, the special value `nil` is returned. Because of this, the operation never fails. ## Examples SET key1 "Hello" SET key2 "World" MGET key1 key2 nonexisting ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of values at the specified keys. **RESP3:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of values at the specified keys.
intro:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.


Returns the values of all specified keys.
For every key that does not hold a string value or does not exist, the special
value `nil` is returned.
Because of this, the operation never fails.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.


Returns the values of all specified keys.
For every key that does not hold a string value or does not exist, the special
value `nil` is returned.
Because of this, the operation never fails.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
SET key1 "Hello"
SET key2 "World"
MGET key1 key2 nonexisting

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of values at the specified keys.

**RESP3:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of values at the specified keys.
